—A clear and honest warning was given to Asa by a prophet of the Lord because he made an agreement with Syria. God is displeased when he is distrusted, and when reliance is placed on human strength rather than his power and goodness. It is foolish to depend on something unreliable when we have the Rock of ages to rely upon. To convince Asa of his error, the prophet pointed out that he, of all people, had no reason to distrust God, who had proven himself a powerful Helper. The many times we have experienced God’s goodness toward us makes our distrust of him even worse. But see how deceptive our hearts are! We trust in God when we have nothing else to trust in, when need drives us to him; but when we have other things to depend on, we tend to rely too much on them. Notice Asa's displeasure at this warning. What is a person when God leaves him to himself? He who misused his power to persecute God’s prophet was left to himself, to misuse it further and oppress his own people. Two years before he died, Asa became ill in his feet. It was his duty to use doctors; but trusting them and expecting from them what should have come from God alone were his sin and folly. In all struggles and suffering we need especially to examine our own hearts, that they may be whole toward God, through faith, patience, and obedience.
